title: Fixing a Node.js Express Middleware Route with Incorrect Route Parameter Validation

statement.markdown: Given a Node.js Express application, write a middleware function to validate the 'id' route parameter in the following route: '/users/:id'. The function should return a 400 Bad Request response if the 'id' parameter is not a positive integer.
statement.notes: The middleware function should be used before the main route handler.

examples.explanation: The 'id' parameter is not a positive integer.
The 'id' parameter is a positive integer.

hints.text: Use the 'params' object in the Express route handler to access the 'id' parameter.

editorial.title: Editorial: Fixing the Route Parameter Validation Middleware
editorial.intuition: The goal is to validate the 'id' parameter before the main route handler is executed.
editorial.bruteForce: Use a regular expression to match the 'id' parameter, but this will not provide the correct validation.
editorial.betterSolution: Use the 'isInteger' function from the 'lodash' library to validate the 'id' parameter.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use the 'Number.isInteger' method to validate the 'id' parameter. This is the most efficient solution.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The 'Number.isInteger' method will return true if the 'id' parameter is a positive integer, and false otherwise.
editorial.dryRun: Example usage of the optimal solution: app.get('/users/:id', validateIdMiddleware, (req, res) => { res.send('Valid 'id' parameter'); });
commonMistakes: Using a regular expression to match the 'id' parameter
